text: Three batch reviews cover 3 PRs: (1) accessibility audit fixes, (2) iteration 3 fixes for audit items, and (3) performance audit fixes. Across ~60 files, the work is generally well-executed with correct patterns for theme tokens, focus rings, motion-safe prefixes, touch targets, and ARIA attributes. However, 1 critical regression (BadgeCard dark-mode contrast), ~15 important issues (missing focus rings, disabled states, aria attributes, hardcoded colors, motion-safe prefixes, and a ProtectedRoute authorization regression), and ~15 minor issues remain. The PRs are close to merge-ready but need targeted fixes.

reasoning: One critical regression (BadgeCard dark-mode contrast, ~2.5:1 ratio) and one important authorization regression (ProtectedRoute substring matching allowing ADMIN to bypass ORGANIZATION_ADMIN route guards). ~15 important and ~15 minor issues remain across all three PRs. All issues are straightforward pattern-based fixes with low regression risk.

message: Complete dismiss implementation: Escape key handler, backdrop click handler, and close button with min-44px touch target — all three dismissal mechanisms properly implemented.
Hardcoded emerald/teal colors correctly replaced with theme tokens (from-brand-primary/90, from-brand-secondary/90) with dark-mode variants.
Correctly fixes a stale closure bug by switching from reviewMutation.variables?.action to variables.action (onSuccess parameter), ensuring toast reflects the actual completed mutation.
Elegant ContentSchema extraction combining .min(1), .max(50000), and HTML-strip refine into a reusable validator shared between CreateBlogPostSchema and UpdateBlogPostSchema.
Extracting inline ThemeToggleButton to an imported ThemeToggle eliminates the React reconciliation problem where a new component type was created on every render.
Emoji medals replaced with Lucide Trophy/Award/Medal SVG icons — follows project conventions and improves accessibility.
All semantic <th> elements now have scope='col'. The Manage action link touch target increased from py-1.5 to py-3 with min-h-[44px].
Container changed from <div> to <nav> with aria-label='Pagination' — proper landmark for screen readers.
Thorough accessibility fix: all 4 fields receive disabled={isSubmitting}, aria-describedby, aria-invalid, and matching error paragraph id attributes.
Replacing z.literal(true) with z.boolean().refine(v => v === true) properly resolves the 'false as never' type hack in consent/page.tsx.
Replacing blanket watch() with per-field watch('frequency'), watch('daysOfWeek'), etc. correctly fixes the useMemo invalidation issue and prevents unnecessary re-renders.
Loading spinner now uses motion-safe:animate-spin — respects prefers-reduced-motion.
Good use of role="status" with descriptive aria-label — screen readers will announce the streak count as a live region update.
Added Escape key handler to close notification panel — good keyboard dismissal pattern.

Dark mode contrast regression: bg-brand-primary maps to #34d399 in dark mode, which is lighter than emerald-500 (#10b981). White text on #34d399 yields ~2.5:1 contrast ratio, below WCAG SC 1.4.11 minimum of 3:1 for non-text content.
suggestion: Use dark-mode-aware styling: earned ? 'bg-brand-primary text-white dark:text-brand-text' : '...' or define a darker dark-mode primary shade maintaining 3:1+ contrast.

message: rolesKey.includes(user.role) performs substring matching instead of exact role comparison. With allowedRoles=['ORGANIZATION_ADMIN'] and user.role='ADMIN', the check would incorrectly return true since 'ADMIN' is a substring of 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN', granting unauthorized access. This is an authorization correctness regression.
suggestion: Keep allowedRoles.includes(user.role) for the membership check but use rolesKey only as the useEffect dependency.

message: Close button (X icon) is missing focus-visible:ring. Keyboard users navigating via Tab get no branded focus indicator.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Still uses hardcoded Tailwind amber colors (bg-amber-100, text-amber-500, etc.) instead of theme tokens.
suggestion: Replace with bg-brand-accent/10 dark:bg-brand-accent/20, text-brand-accent, text-brand-accent/80 dark:text-brand-accent.

message: Label text remains text-[10px] (<11px minimum recommended for accessibility).
suggestion: Bump to text-xs (12px) or text-[11px] with tighter letter-spacing if needed.

message: Cancel button still missing autoFocus attribute. Keyboard users must tab through the entire page to reach dialog controls.
suggestion: Add autoFocus to the Cancel <Button> so the dialog auto-focuses its first interactive element.

message: Partial fix for disabled state during submission: title, description, interval fields are disabled, but 5 interactive element groups remain active — frequency buttons, day-of-week buttons, virtual toggle, end condition radio buttons, and end condition conditional inputs.
suggestion: Add disabled={isSubmitting} or pointer-events-none + opacity-60 to all remaining interactive elements during submission.

message: firstEventDate input lacks disabled={isSubmitting}. Users can modify this field during form submission, risking race conditions.
suggestion: Add disabled={isSubmitting} to the firstEventDate input.

message: capacity input lacks disabled={isSubmitting}. Users can modify capacity during form submission.
suggestion: Add disabled={isSubmitting} to the capacity input.

message: LocationSelect error banner uses hardcoded red colors (bg-red-50, border-red-200, text-red-700) instead of theme tokens.
suggestion: Replace with bg-brand-error/10 border-brand-error/20 text-brand-error matching the pattern used in the server error.

message: Email <input> missing aria-invalid={!!errors.email} and aria-describedby={errors.email ? 'email-error' : undefined}. The name field was correctly fixed but email was missed.
suggestion:
Add aria-invalid and aria-describedby to the email input, linked to the error

element by id.

message: Name error message (line 100) and website error message (line 171) lack role='alert'. The email error has it correctly.
suggestion: Add role='alert' to name and website error message containers.

message: CTA <Link> element still missing focus-visible:ring. Keyboard-focusable element with no visible focus indicator.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Secondary quick-action arrow uses group-hover:translate-x-0.5 transition-colors without motion-safe: prefix. The CTA arrow was fixed but this was missed.
suggestion: Prefix with motion-safe: group-hover:translate-x-0.5 motion-safe:transition-colors.

message: Progress bar uses transition-all duration-500 without motion-safe: prefix.
suggestion: Add motion-safe: prefix to transition classes.

message: 'Remove' certification button uses hardcoded hover:text-red-500 instead of theme token hover:text-brand-error.
suggestion: Replace hover:text-red-500 with hover:text-brand-error.

message: Certification input uses py-2.5 (~36px touch target). Still below 44px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum.
suggestion: Add min-h-[44px] or change to py-3.

message: Add certification button lacks focus-visible:ring for keyboard focus indication.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Error state uses hardcoded red colors (bg-red-100, text-red-500, text-red-600) with added dark: variants instead of migration to theme tokens.
suggestion: Replace with bg-brand-error/10, text-brand-error, text-brand-error/80.

message: Recurring toggle has transition classes without motion-safe: prefix. Does not respect prefers-reduced-motion.
suggestion: Prefix transition classes with motion-safe: on both toggle containers and knob elements.

message: Frequency selection buttons (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/CUSTOM) lack focus-visible:ring for keyboard focus indication.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Day-of-week selection buttons (Sun-Sat) lack focus-visible:ring for keyboard focus indication.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Dropdown menu (role='menu') has no keyboard navigation. WCAG 4.1.2 requires ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Home/End navigation.
suggestion: Add onKeyDown handler for arrow key navigation, or use an accessible menu library.

message: Primary nav item links lack focus-visible:ring classes. The 'More' and close buttons were fixed, but the four main nav links remain without branded focus indicators.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Uses text-destructive (shadcn/ui token) for upload error, while rest of codebase consistently uses text-brand-error. Pre-existing inconsistency.
suggestion: Replace text-destructive with text-brand-error.

message: Cancel and Add buttons in endorsement form lack focus-visible:ring. The adjacent delete button was fixed with min-touch-target and focus ring, but these form action buttons were not updated.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Action column spacer <th> lacks scope='col'. Other header cells were fixed but this one was overlooked.
suggestion: Add scope='col' to the action column <th>.

message: Error message uses text-red-500 instead of theme token text-brand-error.
suggestion: Replace with text-brand-error.

message: CATEGORY_COLORS map uses entirely hardcoded Tailwind color classes instead of theme tokens.
suggestion: Consider using CSS custom properties per category or a theme-aware utility approach.

message: Install button uses focus-visible:ring-white/50 on white (bg-white) background — white-on-white ring is nearly invisible.
suggestion: Use focus-visible:ring-brand-primary or focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2.

message: Dismiss button uses focus-visible:ring-white/50 against brand-primary gradient — ~2.5:1 contrast, below WCAG 3:1 minimum for focus indicators.
suggestion: Use focus-visible:ring-white/70 or focus-visible:ring-white.

message: focus-visible:rounded applies default border-radius while sibling elements use branded rounded values — inconsistent visual appearance.
suggestion: Replace focus-visible:rounded with focus-visible:rounded-sm or focus-visible:rounded-md.

message: Spinner uses border-current/30 border-t-current. On cta variant (bg-brand-cta text-white), spinner is white-on-white with partial transparency — nearly invisible when stationary.
suggestion: Consider a fixed contrast color: border-white/30 border-t-white for all variants on dark backgrounds.

message: transition-colors without motion-safe: prefix on icon variant button (and button variant at line 68).
suggestion: Add motion-safe: prefix to all transition classes.

message: ThemeToggleButton (mounted state) lacks focus-visible:ring while all other interactive elements in this PR received it.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none.

message: Social icon buttons use bg-brand-surface matching footer background — touch target area has no visible container until hover.
suggestion: Add a subtle border like border border-brand-border so the touch target is visually apparent.

message: beforeunload handler fires unconditionally on all navigation attempts rather than only when formState.isDirty is true.
suggestion: Conditionally add/remove the listener based on isDirty from useForm's formState.

message: The checked={field.value as unknown as boolean} cast on the privacy checkbox is now redundant since ConsentSchema uses z.boolean() instead of z.literal(true).
suggestion: Remove the cast: checked={field.value}

message: Button uses both loading={isSubmitting} and inline text 'Sending...' switch. The Button component already handles loading state.
suggestion: Remove conditional children to let the Button's own loading UI handle it.

message: Unsafe as EventSeriesFormData type assertion on a partial object. The cast bypasses TypeScript entirely.
suggestion: Accept Partial<EventSeriesFormData> in calculatePreviewDates or define a narrow interface with only the needed fields.

message: React.ElementType is overly broad — accepts intrinsic elements like 'div' or 'span' which would fail at runtime when rendered as <IconComponent />.
suggestion: Use React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }> instead.
